ucs.title: [LLM] Suckfly Nidiran-style: validly code-signed but fleet-rare dropper spawning credential-dumping + network-scanning hacktools
ucs.description: Hunts Suckfly's signature tradecraft: dropping a backdoor (Nidiran / Backdoor.Nidiran) signed with a code-signing certificate stolen from a legitimate (often South Korean) software vendor, which then executes the group's preferred hacktool kit — credential dumpers (gsecdump, pwdump, Mimikatz) and the Hivelocity/nbtscan-class network sweeper. Catches the chain T1553.002 -> T1059.003 -> T1003 + T1046 that distinguishes Suckfly from generic China-nexus crews.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_name) as process_name values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process IN ("*mimikatz*","*gsecdump*","*pwdump*","*wce.exe*","*procdump*lsass*","*sekurlsa*","*ntds.dit*","*nbtscan*","*hivelocity*","*nmap*","*masscan*","*superscan*","*nbscan*")) by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process_exec Processes.parent_process_path Processes.parent_process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | eval cmd_shell_parent=if(match(parent_process_name,"(?i)cmd\.exe|powershell\.exe"),1,0) | join type=inner parent_process_hash [ | tstats summariesonly=true dc(Endpoint.Processes.dest) as fleet_hosts from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Endpoint.Processes.parent_process_hash | rename Endpoint.Processes.parent_process_hash as parent_process_hash | where fleet_hosts <= 5 ] | lookup local=true signed_dropper_lookup process_hash AS parent_process_hash OUTPUT signer signature_status | where signature_status="Valid" AND NOT match(signer,"(?i)Microsoft|Google|Adobe|Mozilla|Oracle|VMware|Citrix") | stats min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime values(process_name) as hacktools values(dest) as hosts dc(dest) as host_count by parent_process_name parent_process_path parent_process_hash signer fleet_hosts | where host_count >= 1 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let credTools = dynamic(["mimikatz","gsecdump","pwdump","wce.exe","procdump","sekurlsa","ntds.dit","lsass.dmp","comsvcs.dll"]);
let scanTools = dynamic(["nbtscan","hivelocity","nbscan","nmap","masscan","superscan","portscan"]);
let rareSignedBinaries =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessSignatureStatus =~ "Valid"
    | where isnotempty(InitiatingProcessSHA1)
    | summarize fleetHosts = dcount(DeviceId) by InitiatingProcessSHA1
    | where fleetHosts <= 5;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessSHA1 in (rareSignedBinaries)
| where (ProcessCommandLine has_any (credTools) or FileName has_any (credTools)
      or ProcessCommandLine has_any (scanTools) or FileName has_any (scanTools))
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileCertificateInfo
    | summarize arg_max(Timestamp, IsTrusted, Signer, Issuer, IsRootSignerMicrosoft) by SHA1
  ) on $left.InitiatingProcessSHA1 == $right.SHA1
| where IsRootSignerMicrosoft != true
| where Signer !contains "Microsoft" and Signer !contains "Google" and Signer !contains "Adobe" and Signer !contains "Mozilla" and Signer !contains "Oracle" and Signer !contains "VMware" and Signer !contains "Citrix"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA1, Signer, Issuer, IsTrusted, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Suckfly is uniquely characterised in Symantec/Mandiant reporting as the China-nexus actor that systematically stole code-signing certificates from legitimate (mostly South Korean) firms and re-signed Nidiran droppers and hacktools with them. The combination — VALID Authenticode signature + non-mainstream signer + fleet rarity + IMMEDIATE invocation of credential-dump + network-scan tooling — is th

ucs.title: [LLM] Suckfly stolen-credential pivot: cmd.exe-driven internal port/share sweep followed by interactive logon from same host (T1078 + T1059.003 +
ucs.description: Hunts Suckfly's post-exploitation lateral-movement pattern observed at Indian/South Asian government and commercial victims: after credential theft they live off cmd.exe, run a network/SMB sweep from the beachhead, then re-use the harvested valid account to log onto enumerated hosts within minutes. Designed to surface the chain not any one step.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as scan_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="cmd.exe" (Processes.process IN ("*nbtscan*","*hivelocity*","*net view*","*net use*","*for /L*\\\\*","*ping -n 1*","*nmap*","*masscan*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | rename dest as src_host user as scan_user | join type=inner src_host scan_user [ | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Authentication.dest) as logon_targets dc(Authentication.dest) as target_count min(_time) as logon_time from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action="success" Authentication.authentication_method IN ("NTLM","Kerberos") by Authentication.src Authentication.user | `drop_dm_object_name("Authentication")` | rename src as src_host user as scan_user | where target_count >= 3 ] | where logon_time > scan_time AND (logon_time - scan_time) < 1800 | table scan_time logon_time src_host scan_user process logon_targets target_count
ucs.kql:
let scanWindow = 30m;
let scans =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "cmd.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("nbtscan","hivelocity","net view","net use \\","for /L","ping -n 1","nmap","masscan")
          or (FileName =~ "net.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("view","use \\"))
    | project ScanTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName=tolower(AccountName), ScanCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let logons =
    IdentityLogonEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType =~ "LogonSuccess"
    | where LogonType in ("Network","Interactive","RemoteInteractive")
    | project LogonTime=Timestamp, SrcDevice=DeviceName, AccountName=tolower(AccountName), TargetDeviceName, LogonType, Protocol;
scans
| join kind=inner (logons) on $left.DeviceName == $right.SrcDevice, AccountName
| where LogonTime between (ScanTime .. ScanTime + scanWindow)
| summarize TargetsHit=dcount(TargetDeviceName), Targets=make_set(TargetDeviceName, 25), ScanCmds=make_set(ScanCmd, 10), FirstScan=min(ScanTime), LastLogon=max(LogonTime) by DeviceName, AccountName
| where TargetsHit >= 3
| order by FirstScan desc
ucs.rationale: Suckfly's tradecraft (per Symantec March 2016 reporting) is unusually shell-centric for a Chinese state-aligned actor — they prefer cmd.exe + standalone scanner binaries rather than PowerShell or WMI, and they pivot with stolen valid accounts rather than dropping new implants. Tying a cmd.exe-launched network/SMB sweep to a successful logon by the SAME user from the SAME host within 30 minutes aga
